taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about the larynx.
textField:
The *cricoid* cartilage is the only complete ring in the respiratory tract. The larynx extends from vertebral level *C3* to C6. The *thyroid* cartilage is the largest laryngeal cartilage and forms the laryngeal prominence (Adam's apple).

The *posterior cricoarytenoid* is the only muscle that abducts the vocal cords, opening the rima glottidis. Its paralysis results in closure of the airway. The recurrent laryngeal nerve supplies all intrinsic muscles of the larynx except the *cricothyroid* muscle, which is supplied by the *external laryngeal* nerve.

The *cricothyroid* membrane (conus elasticus) is the site for emergency airway access (cricothyrotomy). The free upper edge of the conus elasticus forms the *vocal* ligament. The interior of the larynx is divided into three compartments: vestibule, *ventricle*, and subglottis.

Glottic carcinoma presents early with *hoarseness* because even small tumours affect vocal cord vibration. The narrowest part of the adult larynx is the *rima glottidis*, while in children it is the subglottis at the cricoid ring.

The internal laryngeal nerve and superior laryngeal vessels pierce the *thyrohyoid* membrane to enter the larynx.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Review the details you missed.
